titleStmt.title: Letter from Margaret Donnan to Jane Donnan, 28 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Margaret Donnan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Margaret Donnan, a 50 year old mother to her 24 year old daughter who was working in Post Office in Killyleagh Co Down, urging her to apply for work in the Ballymena Cottage Hospital.
This letter tells of events that Margaret and her 10 year old daughter witnessed from her upstairs window in Castlebellingham Co Louth and information she gathered from neighbours.
It also tells of her husband volunteering to guard the railway bridge with other men from the village. This letter also gives news of two sons that had enlisted and were in France, a 17 year old daughter who had recently gone to work in the post office in England, and another son at home taking food to his father guarding the bridges from being blown up.
The Donnan family were Presbyterian. Members of the family, including Margaret, had worked for the Bellingham family. Margaret was a cook. The family would not have been sympathetic to the Rising.
Enclosed is a very sad photograph. Three years before it was taken, in 1905, they had lost two children, a baby boy aged one year and a little girl who died aged two, just 4 months before the youngest child in the photograph was born.

correspAction.placeName: 43 High Street, Killyleagh, Co. Down, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Castle Bellingham Co Louth April 28 Dear Jane. your card this morng. I hope you have got my letter.
I wrote you on Wed. and sent you an advertisement of Ballamin a
Hospital. It is in this weeks Witness also — asking you to send Apalication
to materon. If you did not get my letter of Wed last, if you
get this one be sure to send applications to the matron
of Ballymina Hospital — here is a Copy of advertisement,
Wanted Probationer for the Ballymena Cottage Hospital
Apply at once with testimonials to the Matron the Cottage
Hospital Ballymena. 6493 Witness Office I hope you will get this. Our letters are going by Bepost. I wrot
to Peg. and sent her money last night. I hope she
will get it. I am sure she knows how they are over here.
We got no letters or Papers since Monday — no Post went out or
in from Dublin. a few from Belfast might come —
they have make arrangements to send by Belfast. Now
on Monday. a lot of Volunteers marched past here to DMG Side
about 4. 2 Police guarding them a brake —Cars — the only went
to Lurgan when the stoped every Motor-Car coming from the
races — and Put the People out of them into a House and
took the Car and Driver. the were all armed. Pistols and
rifles they took 8 motors & several cases — took Kings Horse
out of the break Car and 10s worth of bread 3 lbs worth of Bacon in
the Car in on the road side at Lurgan. got also a
horse from Jamie Ned Langan because he refused. The
shot his arm nearly off. I hear it will be taken off.
Only for Oswild Mc Dowl stoped Carlow and sent him back to DMG
for help — he would have lost his motor or his life.
2 the came up to Bellingham & stoped outside of our House believe
Butterley, Burns — all got out started running away Miss B. goods —
Putting them in the Motors from her they took 6 tins of biscuits 8 tins
of salmon. Bread, Milk from Lotus Cheese from barn. Bread.
when that was over or during the time they were talking the
tingssome of them was buisy the put out Sargant Donavan.
Standing at the rails at the end of the rail opposite Burns
and a fellow stood before the with a Pistol all the time the were
serched. and other Police man from Gilbertstown came up
on Bike to post a letter he was put beside our men, searched
Pistol held up to their Chests. up comes a young officer in
a grand motor Car. Ltf Dunville from Belfast Distillery
whiskey man from Belfast stoped to ordered but put standing
beside the Police also his man Driver. 5 in a row
as helpless as babes. revolvers. & pistols pointed at
them all all sides the officer was going to Kingstown
to catch a boat for France. when they had the shops stacked stacked
they got ready blew up the officer motor tiers shot him self
Two places in the Chest shot the Constable Charles Magee
3 places as much stuff in him as would kill 3 men
when the Shot the Police. man our men run across and in to Burns
and the Driver, Magee . run. to Kelly fell on the road was Draged
in to Kelly archway. the officer remained standing with his
arms up, for mercy. a fellow called out to him hands up
we will not shoot you in Cold Blood the fellow was Dying at
the time and very soon fell forward on his side in a Puddle
of water. and the Sinn Feiners looking on at him falling
shot 2 Places in the Chest. the Motors left them then and
two fellows. ran. and draged him to Kelly. where the Dr.
was Dressing Magees wounds. the Priest was got for Magee sent
to Hospital died at the door of Hospital going in — L
3 On Monday, evening at 7 all this Happened, opposite our
House. I was up stairs looking out all this time seen all —
only got muddled. Hilda was with me Father was just
gone to the cattle gong Joe Hand Caney Carroll Burn me
Lilly Kelly. Sarah Conaton all seen what went on.
everyone on was Powerless afraid to move or thewould
fire on any one like a Dog. the got up and drove
away towards Dublin about 11 the military . Come
back about one. The were gone on in their hurry
they forgot a motor Load of amination in the Street
Bandages water bottles all thing really. So father Willee
Mc Kinney James Carson 'Heron' Lowe all carried the
stuff to the Barrick Pushed the motor to the Barricks
the soulders took It away. at 1 Ocloc in the night
to Dmg. their is 8 of our gang arrested sent to Belfast jail
Mr. Leslie seen them they are Dundalk men. Machtee
Hughes. Karney. — all well known to all. the will leaving
today. On yesterday I had a drive to Dmg. to give experience
at the Hospital. Sarah Conaton. Lily Kelly. me Joe. Carney. Carroll.
when we got down we were not wanted fro some time longer.
Hilda went to in the main time Father all the fellows
had to leave out of the brewry and go guard bridges
Father was at a lot of other from here — and some
souldiers, Cranson. Some and some soldiers at CBH
from Dublin to Dunleen is . and lined up with
souldiers across the . there was word yesterday
the broke out. and was coming down the line Matilda Kelly
. On Monday the took the P.O. Dublin and the could not
got out. the were to gass them out last night. I see in to days Belfast
their is a good many of course the blew up a Bridge
this side of Dublin and that what they are afraid of. 3
4 I need hardly tell you about Dublin as if you see
the Belfast Paper you will see. All. Since
Monday all our men are doing Police work.
Of course theer is no Police all away — our men
was releived by Police at Drumin yesterday
Joe McMahons motor went up with them. a special
came down with them at 10 last night 12 hours
Edward went up with some grub to Father yesterday
to Drumin he was stoped by
Dundalk. men are guarding Stabannon
after some time you get searched. and let pass on
Oswald McDowl is a Dispatch rider from Dundalk
to Dunleer reporting from one to the
other barriers how things are getting on.
in the streets of Dundalk. Yesterday the were
lorry drew across them one care at a
time can only pass — did you see where
Sir Roger Casement came in in the west of Ireland
to help the Sinn Feiners. I see a bit in the Belfast
Paper. Mrs W. sends us down to Belfast paper for 5 minutes
in the morning to have a look. this is all we see —
so content yourself if you do not hear from us.
no word of the boys lately of course. I am not annoyed.
the are on the road some place — Good by —
you know the wires are all cut. 15 motor loads of police
went by to Drogheda on Wednesday Mother Donnan
Page 2 they came up to Bellingham stopped outside of our house below(?) Butterly, Burns
(these were the names of shops) all got out started running away ? ? goods putting
them in motors from her they took 6 tins of biscuits, 8 tins of salmon, bread milk
from ? Toties cheese from ? Bread When that was over or during the time they
taking the things some of them was busy(?) they put our Sergeant Donovan standing
at tha rails at the end of the rail opposite Burns and a fellow stood before them with
a pistol all the time they were searched and another police man from Gilbertstown
came up on bike to post a letter he was put beside our men searched, pistol held up
to their chests, --- up comes a young officer in a grand motor car. Lft Dunville from
Belfast Distillery whisky man from Belfast stopped to order(ed) but put standing
beside the police also his man Driver. 5 in a row as helpless as babes. revolvers and
pistols pointed at them at all sides the officer was going to Kingstown to catch a
boat for France when the had ??? ??? they got ready blew up the officers motor
tyres shot himself two places in the chest shot the Constable Charles Magee 3 places
as much stuff in him as would kill 3 men when they shot the police man our men ran across and into Burns and the Driver,
Magee run to Kelly fell on the road was dragged into Kelly archway. the officer
remained standing with his arms up --for mercy. a fellow called out to him, "hands
up we will no shoot you in cold blood" the fellow was dying at the time and very
soon fell forward on his side in a puddle of water and the Sinn Fieners looking on at
him falling shot 2 places in the chest the motors left then and two fellows ran and
dragged him to Kelly where the Dr was dressing Magee's wounds the priest was
got to Magee sent to hospital died at the door going in
Page 2 they came up to Bellingham stopped outside of our house below(?) Butterly, Burns
(these were the names of shops) all got out started running away ? ? goods putting
them in motors from her they took 6 tins of biscuits, 8 tins of salmon, bread milk
from ? Toties cheese from ? Bread When that was over or during the time they
taking the things some of them was busy(?) they put our Sergeant Donovan standing
at tha rails at the end of the rail opposite Burns and a fellow stood before them with
a pistol all the time they were searched and another police man from Gilbertstown
came up on bike to post a letter he was put beside our men searched, pistol held up
to their chests, --- up comes a young officer in a grand motor car. Lft Dunville from
Belfast Distillery whisky man from Belfast stopped to order(ed) but put standing
beside the police also his man Driver. 5 in a row as helpless as babes. revolvers and
pistols pointed at them at all sides the officer was going to Kingstown to catch a
boat for France when the had ??? ??? they got ready blew up the officers motor
tyres shot himself two places in the chest shot the Constable Charles Magee 3 places
as much stuff in him as would kill 3 men when they shot the police man our men ran across and into Burns and the Driver,
Magee run to Kelly fell on the road was dragged into Kelly archway. the officer
remained standing with his arms up --for mercy. a fellow called out to him, "hands
up we will no shoot you in cold blood" the fellow was dying at the time and very
soon fell forward on his side in a puddle of water and the Sinn Fieners looking on at
him falling shot 2 places in the chest the motors left then and two fellows ran and
dragged him to Kelly where the Dr was dressing Magee's wounds the priest was
got to Magee sent to hospital died at the door going in
Page 3 On Monday evening at 7 all this happened opposite our house I was up stairs
looking out all the time, seen all only got muddled. Hilda was with me Father was
just gone to the cattle. Johnny Hand (taxi driver) Joe Cerney Carroll Burn me Lily
Kelly Sarah Conaton all seen what went on everyone one was powerlee afraid to
move or they would fire on anyone -- like a dog they got up and drove away
towards Dublin about 11 the military??? come back about one they gone on in
their hurry they forgot a motor load of ammunition in the street Bandages water
bottles all things ready so Father Willie McKinny James Crason Aeron Tome all
carried the stuff to the Barracks Pushed the motor to the Barracks the soldiers took
it away at 1 o'clock in the night to Dmg there is 8 of our gang arrested sent to
Belfast Jail Mr Leslie (Canon Leslie C of I minister) seen them They are Dundalk men
Machtree (McAtee later an Irish government minister) Hughes Karney all well
known to all They will swing surely And yesterday I had a drive to Dundalk to
give evidence at the hospital Sarah Conaton Lily Kelly me Jo Carney Carroll when
we got down we were not wanted for some time longer. Hilda went to Hosies in
the meantime Father was at Drumin a lot of others from here and some soldiers
Cranson Tome and some soldiers at Castlebellingham Station from Dublin to
Dunleer is? trenched and lined up with soldier's lorry across the road. there was
word yesterday that? broke out and was coming down the line Matilda Kelly
wrote home yesterday come to Drogheda took the train down on Monday the
??Sinn took the PC Dulin"? and they could not be got out they were to gas them out
last night. I see in To-days Belfast (paper?) there is a good many dead of course
they blew up a bridge this side of Dublin and that is what they are afraid of.
Page 4 I need hardly tell you about Dublin as if you see Belfast paper you will see all
Since Monday all our men are doing police work of course there is no police all
away-- our men was relieved by police at Dreemin ? yesterday Joe McMahons
motor went up with them a special came down with them at 10 last night 12 hours Edward went up with
some grub to Father yesterday to Drumin he was stopped by Dundalk. men are
guarding Stabanon after some time you get searched and let pass on
Oswald McDowell is a dispatch rider from Dundalk to Dunleer reporting from one
to the other barriers how things are getting on in the streets of Dundalk. Yesterday
there was a lorry drawn across them one car at a time can only pass did you see
where Sir Roger Casement came in the west of Ireland to help the Sinn Feinners.
I see a bit in the Belfast paper. Mrs W.(Auntie Jane Wilson nee Donnan shop keeper
postmistress) sends us down the Belfast paper for 5 minutes in the morning to have
a look this is all we see — so content yourself if you do not hear from us No
word from the boys lately of course I am not annoyed they are on the road some
place —Goodbye —you know the wires are all cut 15 motor loads of police
went by to Drogheda on Wednesday Mother Donnan
